assumptions: The intragroup dividend has already been declared, so the dividend receivable/payable is a recognised monetary item rather than a forecast intragroup transaction.

The entities involved have different functional currencies and the related exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation, so the foreign currency risk affects consolidated profit or loss.
Any hedge designation being assessed would otherwise satisfy the IFRS 9 documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements.

approaches.label: Hedge of foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 expressly permits the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to be a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when the exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 provide a separate consolidated-level hedge accounting route for foreign currency risk on the net assets of a foreign operation, making it a relevant alternative FX hedge designation to consider alongside the distinct receivable exposure.
